title: Broad Mean-Reversion Residual Cap
hypothesis: A smaller, higher-threshold mean-reversion branch with benchmark-bucket caps can test whether the family only becomes viable when the basket is materially more selective and better balanced across benchmark buckets.
why_different_from_retired: The retired broad mean-reversion branches were either slower or broader, while this branch cuts the basket to six names, raises the entry threshold, and adds explicit benchmark-bucket caps in one bounded family.
This is not a replay of the active fast-cycle branch; it keeps the faster recycle concept but materially changes selection strictness and bucket balance.
success_criteria: The residual-cap seed must pass the existing validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion policy without exceptions before it can move toward paper-trade preparation.
If it fails, it must still produce enough evidence to decide whether selective, bucket-balanced mean reversion deserves any backlog space after the current fast-cycle branch.

stop_conditions.when: The residual-cap seed passes validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.

bootstrap.objective: Run one smaller, higher-threshold mean-reversion branch that forces benchmark-bucket balance before the family is retired for good.
bootstrap.branch_rationale: The active fast-cycle branch made mean reversion faster and more diversified, but it still left open whether the family only works when the basket is materially smaller and forced across benchmark buckets. This branch is the bounded test for that hypothesis.
positive_hypotheses: A smaller six-name basket with a stronger residual threshold can strip out weak mean-reversion candidates that diluted the earlier broad attempts.
Benchmark-bucket caps can keep the family from collapsing into one valuation bucket when mean reversion appears strongest in a narrow market pocket.

seed_stack.label: Fast-cycle control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference control for the currently active faster mean-reversion branch.

seed_stack.label: Residual-cap seed
seed_stack.rationale: Repo-backed smaller-basket mean-reversion branch with stronger entry and explicit benchmark-bucket balance.

campaign_path.label: Mean-Reversion Residual-Cap Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the selective mean-reversion family and decide whether it should be promoted or retired.
